Title: MdNAC104 positively regulates apple cold tolerance via CBF-dependent and CBF-independent pathways

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in transgenic apple; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by cold and further elevated in MdNAC104-overexpressing plants
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdCBF1 promoter in reporter assays; 35S promoter for MdNAC104 effector
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates downstream COR pathway to enhance cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdNAC104 directly activates MdCBF1 expression as part of the CBF-dependent cold-response pathway
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCBF1 transcript levels were elevated in MdNAC104-overexpressing apple under cold stress. 2. MdNAC104 directly bound the MdCBF1 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. MdNAC104 activated MdCBF1 promoter activity under cold conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a downstream regulatory target rather than the main focal gene of the paper.

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in transgenic apple; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by cold and further elevated in MdNAC104-overexpressing plants
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MdCBF3 promoter in reporter assays; 35S promoter for MdNAC104 effector
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Activates downstream COR pathway to enhance cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdNAC104 directly activates MdCBF3 expression in apple under cold stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCBF3 expression increased in MdNAC104-overexpressing apple under cold. 2. MdNAC104 directly bound the MdCBF3 promoter. 3. MdNAC104 activated MdCBF3 promoter activity under low temperature.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a downstream regulatory target rather than the main focal gene of the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key early pathway step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; metabolomics; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdCHS-b promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and enhanced cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment at 4°C; prolonged low temperature for anthocyanin measurement
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher MdCHS-b expression contributes to increased anthocyanin accumulation under cold conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCHS-b was among the anthocyanin biosynthetic genes significantly upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing apple under cold stress. 2. MdNAC104 activated the MdCHS-b promoter in dual-luciferase assays. 3. EMSA and ChIP-qPCR showed direct MdNAC104 binding to the MdCHS-b promoter.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; NCBI BioProject PRJNA892233
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen as one of the most upregulated anthocyanin-pathway genes for direct validation.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key early pathway step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; metabolomics; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdCHI-a promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and enhanced cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoted MdCHI-a expression contributes to anthocyanin accumulation under low temperature
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdCHI-a expression increased in MdNAC104-overexpressing plants under cold. 2. MdNAC104 activated the MdCHI-a promoter. 3. Direct binding was supported by EMSA and ChIP-qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; NCBI BioProject PRJNA892233

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway step; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; metabolomics; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdF3H-a promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and enhanced cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased MdF3H-a expression supports anthocyanin biosynthesis and accumulation during cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdF3H-a was significantly upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing apple under cold conditions. 2. MdNAC104 enhanced MdF3H-a promoter activity. 3. EMSA and ChIP-qPCR showed direct promoter binding by MdNAC104.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; NCBI BioProject PRJNA892233
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text once mentions MdF3H-b promoter in EMSA description, but figure and core conclusion indicate MdF3H-a as validated target.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant/late pathway step; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; metabolomics; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdANS-b promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging and enhanced cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhanced MdANS-b expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation during cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdANS-b expression was significantly increased in MdNAC104-overexpressing apple under cold. 2. MdNAC104 activated the MdANS-b promoter. 3. Direct promoter binding was validated by EMSA and ChIP-qPCR.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Dismutates superoxide radicals to hydrogen peroxide and oxygen.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for protection of target metabolite/system by ROS scavenging rather than direct synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; antioxidant enzyme activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdFSD2 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limits oxidative damage and enhances cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SOD activity and ROS removal under cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdFSD2 was among the most strongly upregulated antioxidant enzyme genes in MdNAC104-overexpressing plants. 2. MdNAC104 directly bound the MdFSD2 promoter in vitro and in vivo. 3. MdNAC104 activated MdFSD2 promoter activity, consistent with increased SOD activity and lower ROS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a nutrient-biosynthetic enzyme, it was experimentally validated as a core direct target affecting antioxidant metabolism under cold stress.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hydrogen peroxide using electron-donating substrates as part of peroxidase-mediated ROS detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for protection of target metabolite/system by ROS scavenging rather than direct synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; antioxidant enzyme activity assay
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE lines; callus 'Orin' used for ChIP platform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in MdNAC104-overexpressing leaves under cold treatment
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for MdNAC104 effector/overexpression; native MdPRXR1.1 promoter in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limits oxidative damage and enhances cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes POD activity and ROS detoxification under cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPRXR1.1 was one of the most strongly upregulated POD genes in MdNAC104-overexpressing plants. 2. MdNAC104 directly bound the MdPRXR1.1 promoter. 3. MdNAC104 activated MdPRXR1.1 promoter activity, consistent with higher POD activity and reduced ROS under cold conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cold-response signaling; anthocyanin biosynthesis; antioxidant defense; proline accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator integrating CBF-dependent and CBF-independent cold tolerance pathways; directly activates multiple cold, anthocyanin, and antioxidant genes.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdCBF1; MdCBF3; MdCHS-b; MdCHI-a; MdF3H-a; MdANS-b; MdFSD2; MdPRXR1.1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription of MdCBF1 and MdCBF3; activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes MdCHS-b, MdCHI-a, MdF3H-a, MdANS-b; activates antioxidant genes MdFSD2 and MdPRXR1.1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in apple; physiological assays; RNA-seq; metabolomics; dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: GL-3; transgenic OE#1/OE#2/OE#3; callus 'Orin' for ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: GL-3-derived gene; callus 'Orin' used for some assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by cold, peaking within 3 h
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by cold treatment in apple plants and promoter activity increased by cold
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold acclimation and freezing; chilling at 4°C
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Flavonoid; amino acid-derived osmoprotectant; stress-protective redox traits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS scavenging, osmoprotection, and enhanced cold tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances apple cold tolerance, increases anthocyanin and proline accumulation, elevates antioxidant enzyme activity, reduces ROS and membrane damage
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No interaction detected with tested anthocyanin regulators MdMYB1; MdbHLH3; MdbHLH33; MdMYC2; MdHY5 in Y2H
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No validated protein interactors reported; no Y2H interaction with MdMYB1; MdbHLH3; MdbHLH33; MdMYC2; MdHY5
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression and effector constructs; native promoters of targets in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering cold-tolerant apple with enhanced anthocyanin accumulation and stronger antioxidant capacity
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq and UPLC-MS/MS metabolomics; NCBI BioProject PRJNA892233
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNAC104 is rapidly induced by cold and its overexpression improves freezing tolerance in apple. 2. It directly activates MdCBF1 and MdCBF3, supporting a CBF-dependent cold-response pathway. 3. It also directly activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and antioxidant enzyme genes, increasing anthocyanin accumulation, ROS scavenging, and cold tolerance via CBF-independent pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally cloned as MdNAC1 (MF401514.1; MD15G1415700) in prior work and renamed MdNAC104 to distinguish it from another published MdNAC1 locus.
